overview.description: AI-powered industrial maintenance software for manufacturing and factory operations
overview.description_long: Pulsar Solutions builds AI tools for industrial maintenance — including a voice AI assistant, knowledge management platform, AI-guided maintenance revisions, and automated shift summaries. Focused on reducing human error, preserving know-how from experienced workers, and enabling hands-free maintenance guidance. Supports speech-to-text in Slovak, Czech, and German. Targets B2B clients in manufacturing, aviation, defense, and other industrial sectors.
overview.tagline: Optimize manufacturing with innovative AI-driven solutions

history.origin_story: Founded in Prague to address industrial maintenance challenges through AI — focusing on knowledge loss from retiring workers, increasing equipment complexity, and human error in manufacturing environments.

key_milestones.event: Presented at Forum údržby (Forum of Maintenance) in Prague
Exhibited at 6th Bavarian-Czech Entrepreneurs' Day in Deggendorf
Presented at Forum of Practical Maintenance in Trnava, Slovakia
Presented at Manufacturing Management 2025 in Zilina, Slovakia

risk_assessment.agi_displacement_rationale: Core product is a layer on top of LLMs and voice AI for industrial maintenance — general-purpose foundation models with voice capabilities (e.g. GPT-4o, Gemini) could replicate the basic knowledge management and voice assistant features. Industrial specialization (Slovak/Czech/German speech) and domain-specific workflows provide modest differentiation but limited proprietary data moat.
risk_assessment.market_risk_rationale: Industrial AI maintenance is a real and growing market (Industry 4.0/5.0), but it is competitive with established players like PTC, Augmentir, and Sight Machine. Pulsar targets a human-centric niche with voice guidance which provides differentiation, but the addressable market in Czech/Slovak manufacturing is limited without significant international expansion.
risk_assessment.funding_risk_rationale: No disclosed funding rounds and appears to be at pilot/early-customer stage based on the 'Pilot clients' section. With a team of ~6 and no known revenue or investment, runway risk is significant. T-Mobile and MVA Academy partnerships suggest early-stage support but not institutional venture funding.
risk_assessment.overall_risk_rationale: Early-stage startup with no disclosed funding, a small team, and a product category susceptible to commoditization by general LLM providers. Industrial niche and multi-language support provide some moat, but the company must secure funding and enterprise customer traction quickly to survive.

metadata.notes: Small early-stage startup. Core team of 6 listed on About page. Backed by T-Mobile as strategic tech partner and MVA Academy as innovation partner. Active in Czech/Slovak/German manufacturing conferences 2024-2025. Address: Thámova 7, 186 00 Praha. Contact: svetlana.margetova@pulsar-solutions.com.
